critic_findings.message: mkdirSync before atomicWriteFileSync is redundant: atomicWriteFileSync->writeAndRename already calls mkdirSync(dirname(path), {recursive:true}) at line 22 of atomic-write.ts. The guard can never do useful work.

The schemaVersion-mismatch test (line 46-59) uses writeFileSync to corrupt the file after writeSnapshot. It imports writeFileSync from the test's own import list, not from the module under test — acceptable — but the test name says 'returns null when schemaVersion mismatches' without clarifying which direction. A schemaVersion of 0 (or missing) is an equally important case. One additional short test for schemaVersion: undefined would make the mismatch contract complete.

findings_details.title: jq-absent abort path writes malformed marker JSON
findings_details.body: When jq is not installed, preflight() calls abort(), which calls write_marker(), which runs printf '%s' "$reason" | jq -R -s '.'. Because jq is absent, the subshell produces no output, so MARKER_PATH receives a JSON object with an empty 'reason' field: {"schemaVersion":1,"at":"...","step":"preflight","reason":}. The marker file is malformed and will fail any JSON parser that reads it later. The abort message IS logged, so the user can recover from the log, but the marker is the persistent artefact that GET /api/admin/auto-update/status returns. Fix: replace the jq-R-s pipeline in write_marker with a printf-based JSON escape so write_marker has no jq dependency, or write the reason as a plain unquoted key when jq is absent.

findings_details.title: IFS='|' split truncates reason when reason contains '|'
findings_details.body: The refactored parse_replay_result loop uses IFS='|' read -r branch_key reason to split jq output. The reason field comes from auto-update-replay.ts and can be 'create_failed: <error message>' where the error message is an arbitrary string caught from a rejected Promise. If that message contains a pipe character (e.g. 'create_failed: ENOENT: no such file or directory'), IFS splitting will assign everything up to the first pipe to branch_key and truncate the rest of reason. The logged warning would be misleading. Fix: use a tab delimiter which does not appear in repo slugs, branch names, or typical error messages. Change jq output to: '.failed[]? | [(.branchKey // "(unknown)"), (.reason // "(unknown)")] | @tsv' and the read to: IFS=$'\t' read -r branch_key reason

findings_details.title: pm2_start_c3 uses REPO_ROOT instead of MAIN_CHECKOUT
findings_details.body: pm2_start_c3 passes $REPO_ROOT/ecosystem.config.cjs to pm2 start. When bin/auto-update runs from a worktree (e.g. via launchd if symlinked), REPO_ROOT is the worktree path, not the main checkout. The comment on line 71 explicitly says 'All commands run against MAIN_CHECKOUT'. restart_old_c3 (line 78) has the same bug. Both should use $MAIN_CHECKOUT.

findings_details.title: restart_old_c3 and pm2_start_c3 use REPO_ROOT not MAIN_CHECKOUT for ecosystem config
findings_details.body: Lines 78 and 105: both pm2 invocations pass $REPO_ROOT/ecosystem.config.cjs. The function comment block on lines 71-72 explicitly states 'All commands run against MAIN_CHECKOUT (not $REPO_ROOT) so the script is safe when invoked from a worktree.' When launchd invokes bin/auto-update from the symlinked main checkout REPO_ROOT==MAIN_CHECKOUT, but when manually invoked from a worktree REPO_ROOT points to the worktree and ecosystem.config.cjs may not exist there. Fix: replace $REPO_ROOT with $MAIN_CHECKOUT in both pm2 start calls in restart_old_c3 and pm2_start_c3.

findings_details.title: check_main_clean silently passes when git status fails
findings_details.body: dirty="$(git ... | grep -v '^??' || true)". The '|| true' applies to the entire pipeline including git. If git status fails (bad cwd, corrupted repo), the error is swallowed, dirty is empty, and the check passes incorrectly — the update proceeds on a repo in an unknown state. Fix: capture git output to a temp file, check exit code explicitly before grepping, then clean up.

findings_details.title: jq hard dependency not verified in preflight
findings_details.body: write_marker (line 57) and call_drain_endpoint (lib/auto-update-phases.sh:31) both invoke jq. If jq is not installed, write_marker silently fails (abort calls it with || true), so failure markers are never written. The preflight phase checks for pm2 but never for jq. Fix: add a jq availability check in preflight alongside the pm2 check, or add a top-level dependency check block at the start of bin/auto-update.

findings_details.title: sed delimiter | can be broken by repo paths containing |
findings_details.body: render_plist uses sed -e 's|{{REPO_PATH}}|$repo_path|g'. If repo_path contains the | character the sed expression breaks and produces malformed XML. plutil -lint would catch the broken plist but with an opaque error. Fix: use a delimiter that cannot appear in a filesystem path, such as @ or #.

findings_details.title: s.branchKey as string is an unsafe cast; use a type predicate in the filter instead
findings_details.body: captureActiveSessions filters by typeof s.branchKey === 'string' then casts s.branchKey as string in .map(). TypeScript cannot narrow the type across the filter/map boundary without a type predicate. The cast is safe at runtime but hides type information. Fix: replace the inline filter+map with a filter using a type predicate: .filter((s): s is TerminalSession & { branchKey: string } => typeof s.branchKey === 'string' && s.branchKey.length > 0), then s.branchKey is narrowed to string in .map() without a cast.

findings_details.title: CACHED_C3_SHA IIFE compressed onto one line — extract to a named function
findings_details.body: The IIFE is 113 characters on one line combining variable declaration, try/catch, execFileSync call, and .trim(). This violates the readability expectation of the 50-line function rule (the logic is dense and inlined). Fix: extract to a named module-level function resolveC3Sha(): string { try { return execFileSync(...).trim(); } catch { return 'unknown'; } } then const CACHED_C3_SHA = resolveC3Sha();

findings_details.title: c3Sha: () => string in AutoUpdateRoutesDeps adds indirection without benefit
findings_details.body: CACHED_C3_SHA is a fixed string captured at boot. The interface wraps it as a function so the route handler calls c3Sha() to get the value. This adds indirection with no benefit — the value never changes after boot. A plain string field c3Sha: string is clearer and equally testable (pass 'abc1234' in tests). Fix: change the interface field to c3Sha: string, and the wiring in index.ts to c3Sha: CACHED_C3_SHA.

findings_details.title: Hardcoded /tmp/wt as cwd in fake terminal manager — use homeDir for determinism
findings_details.body: makeFakeTerminalManager returns cwd: '/tmp/wt' (line 33). The replay test at line 205-220 uses homeDir as the cwd so existsSync(session.cwd) returns true. If tests ever use the fake manager's sessions for replay, they would fail because /tmp/wt likely does not exist. Fix: use homeDir as the cwd in makeFakeTerminalManager, or document that these two paths intentionally differ.

findings_details.title: parse_replay_result spawns N+2 jq processes — replace with single jq invocation
findings_details.body: The while loop at lines 157-163 calls jq twice per failed session (once for branchKey, once for reason), spawning N+2 total jq processes. A single jq invocation can produce all failure lines: while IFS='|' read -r branch_key reason; do log "WARN: replay failed for branchKey=$branch_key reason=$reason"; done < <(jq -r '.failed[]? | "\(.branchKey // "(unknown)")" + "|" + ((.reason // "(unknown)")") ' "$body_file" 2>/dev/null). This also removes the redundant inner condition check at line 165 (failed_count > 0 is already the outer guard).

findings_details.title: Redundant failed_count > 0 check nested inside failed_count > 0 guard
findings_details.body: Line 165: 'if [ "$replayed" = "0" ] && [ "$failed_count" -gt 0 ]' is inside 'if [ "$failed_count" -gt 0 ]' (line 155). The [ "$failed_count" -gt 0 ] sub-condition is always true at that point. Fix: simplify to 'if [ "$replayed" = "0" ]'.

findings_details.title: PATH in plist may not include npm or node — hardcoded paths miss nvm/volta installs
findings_details.body: The plist sets PATH=/usr/local/bin:/opt/homebrew/bin:/usr/bin:/bin. Operators who install Node.js via nvm or volta have node/npm/pm2 at ~/.nvm/versions/node/<ver>/bin or ~/.volta/bin, which are not in this PATH. bin/auto-update calls pm2, npm, git, curl, jq — all would fail silently or with 'command not found' under launchd if the operator uses nvm/volta. Fix: bin/install-auto-update should resolve the actual paths to pm2, npm, and node at install time and embed them in the plist, or document this as a known limitation requiring nvm users to symlink binaries to /usr/local/bin.
